provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'If you want the job, you'll have to apply immediately' — 'you'll' tokenizes untaught, so changed to 'you have to ... right away' (right away in-bank g4-u02). d avoids 'formal' (untaught) using 'careful, official way'.

items.d: To ask in an official way for a job you want.
items.s: If you want the job, you have to ___ for it right away.
items.hintDe: Sich um einen Job bemühen, den man haben will.

provenance.note: SB master example 'He's a well-known recording artist' uses untaught 'known'; carrier reframed around a singer/concert (both in-bank). NB the blank also accepts 'autograph' grammatically, so the definition disambiguates to the music-maker sense.

items.d: Somebody who makes music or pictures, or who plays in a band.
items.s: After the concert, people waited to ask the singer for an ___.
items.hintDe: Macht Musik, Bilder oder andere schöne Werke.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'How many people attended the baseball game?' — 'baseball/game' untaught, so reframed to 'More than 500 people ___ the concert last Friday' (concert in-bank). d uses 'show/meeting/school' (in-bank), avoiding 'event' (untaught).

items.d: To go to a show, a meeting or a school.
items.s: More than 500 people ___ the concert last Friday.
items.hintDe: Zu einer Veranstaltung oder in die Schule gehen.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'I saw Madonna and asked her for her autograph' with the proper noun swapped to 'the artist' and 'saw' (untaught) dropped via an 'At the concert' frame. d avoids untaught 'name/signature' using 'signs a card for you to keep'.

items.d: When a famous star signs a card for you to keep.
items.s: At the concert, I asked the artist for her ___.
items.hintDe: Wenn ein Star für dich seinen Namen aufschreibt.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'There was a poor beggar on the street who asked me for money' — 'poor' untaught, so dropped. d uses 'no money/other people/coins' (in-bank), avoiding 'poor'/'person'.

items.d: Somebody on the street with no money who asks people to help them.
items.s: There was a ___ on the street who asked me for money.
items.hintDe: Arme Person, die auf der Straße um Geld bittet.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB editor's letter ('With best wishes from me and the team'); 'team' is untaught, so swapped to 'the whole family'.

items.d: A nice ending you write at the foot of a card or letter to a friend.
items.s: With ___ from me and the whole family, enjoy your holidays!
items.hintDe: Steht am Ende einer Karte oder eines Briefes an Freunde.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'She reads the newspaper on Sunday morning to catch up on the news' (trimmed 'morning'). Three-word blank for the full phrasal verb. d probe-confirmed fully in-bank.

items.d: To do work or jobs that you did not have time to do before.
items.s: She reads the newspaper on Sunday to ___ the news.
items.hintDe: Etwas Versäumtes später erledigen.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'I feel really tired after the race. I need to chill out at home for a few hours' — 'few/hours' untaught, so tail changed to 'and do nothing'. d uses 'take it easy' (in-bank g2-u07) to avoid untaught 'relax'.

items.d: To take it easy and stop work, often at home, when you feel tired.
items.s: I feel really tired after the race. I need to ___ at home and do nothing.
items.hintDe: Nach einem anstrengenden Tag zu Hause runterkommen.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'We burn oil, wood or coal to heat our homes in winter' — 'wood/heat/winter' untaught, so trimmed to 'We burn oil or ___ to make a fire at home'. d avoids 'hard' (untaught).

items.d: A black rock from the ground that you burn to make a fire.
items.s: We burn oil or ___ to make a fire at home.
items.hintDe: Schwarzes Gestein, das man zum Heizen verbrennt.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'To be successful in any sport, you need skill and discipline' — 'sport'(sg.)/'skill' untaught, so reframed to 'good at any sports ... a lot of ___'. d uses 'control'/'working' (probe-confirmed in-bank).

items.d: When you control what you do and keep at your work in a good way.
items.s: To be good at any sports, you need a lot of ___.
items.hintDe: Selbstkontrolle: dranbleiben und ordentlich arbeiten.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from the SB editor's letter opening ('Welcome to the new edition of FLY HIGH, the school magazine of Five Oaks Middle School').

items.d: One copy of a magazine, newspaper or book that is new at one time.
items.s: Welcome to the new ___ of FLY HIGH, the school magazine.
items.hintDe: Eine bestimmte Nummer einer Zeitschrift oder Zeitung.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'My elder brother is 19. I'm 15' — 'brother' is untaught, so changed to 'sister' (in-bank). d uses 'brother or sister' in-bank words; 'brother' is untaught -> reworded; probe-confirmed.

items.d: More years old than you; used about a sister in a family.
items.s: My ___ sister is 19. I am 15.
items.hintDe: So nennst du z. B. deine ältere Schwester.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'This is too dangerous. I am not getting involved.' with 'with it' added to exercise the full three-particle phrase as the blank. d avoids untaught 'activity/risky' using 'take part in a group ... of bad people' (all in-bank).

items.d: To start to take part in a group, often a group of bad people.
items.s: This is too dangerous. I am not ___ it.
items.hintDe: Bei etwas (oft Riskantem) mitmachen.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from the SB master example 'I sometimes help out in the kitchen.' d avoids 'someone' (untaught) using 'somebody' (in-bank g1-u05).

items.d: To do work for somebody for a short time when they need it.
items.s: I sometimes ___ in the kitchen.
items.hintDe: Kurz mit anpacken, wenn jemand Hilfe braucht.

provenance.note: Carrier built from the SB Guilfest text ('Here's my highlight from last July ... went to the Guilfest'); reframed with the gerund 'Going to' to keep the blank clean. d avoids 'part' (untaught) using 'bit'.

items.d: The best and most exciting time of a day or a show.
items.s: Going to the Guilfest was the ___ of my July.
items.hintDe: Der beste, schönste Teil von etwas.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from the SB master example 'I didn't know. Honestly, I didn't.' (sentence-initial blank; full answer is naturally capitalised). d avoids the headword and uses 'true/lie' (in-bank).

items.d: In a true way; you really do not tell a lie.
items.s: I didn't know. ___, I didn't.
items.hintDe: Ganz ehrlich, ohne zu lügen.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'We killed time watching the boats on the river' — 'boats' untaught, so reframed to 'Our train did not come, so we ___ reading magazines' (single blank, answer 'killed time'). d avoids untaught 'something' with 'a fun thing'; does not leak 'kill'/'time'.

items.d: To do a fun thing while you wait, to make the wait feel shorter.
items.s: Our train did not come, so we ___ reading magazines.
items.hintDe: Etwas tun, damit das Warten schneller vergeht.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from the SB master example 'That was today's news. And now, last but not least – the weather' (trimmed to one clause). d avoids 'final/others/important' (untaught) using 'final thing on a list' — 'final' untaught, reworded and probe-checked.

items.d: You use this just before the end, to show it is good too.
items.s: And now, ___, here is the weather.
items.hintDe: Sagst du vor dem letzten Punkt, der genauso wichtig ist.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'The legend tells us that one day King Arthur will return ...' — 'tells/return' untaught here, so reframed around 'The old ___ of King Arthur is a story everyone in Britain loves' (King Arthur, Britain are harvested proper nouns). d is fully in-bank (probe ok).

items.d: A very old story that may be true or not true.
items.s: The old ___ of King Arthur is a story everyone in Britain loves.
items.hintDe: Sehr alte Geschichte, die wahr sein kann oder nicht.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB Guilfest text 'There's a big leisure centre nearby. So having a shower's not a problem' — 'problem' untaught, so tail swapped to 'with a gym and a sports hall'.

items.d: A public building in a town where you can do sports, go swimming and have fun.
items.s: There's a big ___ nearby with a gym and a sports hall.
items.hintDe: Öffentliches Gebäude mit Sporthalle, Pool usw.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'She's making a lot of money with her job.' — reworked to a single blank ('She's ___ with her job ...', answer 'making money') so the whole phrase is one fill. d must not leak the headword token 'money' (V-8), so uses 'earn good pay ... food and books'.

items.d: To earn good pay from a job or from selling food and books.
items.s: She's ___ with her job and saving up for a car.
items.hintDe: Mit Arbeit oder Verkauf Geld bekommen.

provenance.note: Carrier built from the SB Guilfest line 'it's nice to have something else to do occasionally' and the master example ('We go there occasionally in the summer'); reframed for a clean contrastive blank.

items.d: Not every day, but sometimes; now and then.
items.s: We don't go there every day, but we go ___ in the summer.
items.hintDe: Nicht oft, nur manchmal.

provenance.note: Carrier verbatim from the SB master example 'I love strawberries, but they must be ripe.' d avoids untaught 'grown/fruit' using 'ready to eat, soft and not green' with in-bank words.

items.d: Ready to eat because it is not green any more; used about food on a tree.
items.s: I love strawberries, but they must be ___.
items.hintDe: Wenn Obst fertig zum Essen ist.

provenance.note: SB master example is 'Last year we went scuba-diving in California' but 'went' is untaught; reframed to a 'great place for ___ in the sea' carrier keeping California. d uses 'breathe' (taught at g4-u07 'survival skills' set / probe-confirmed in-bank), so no gloss is needed.

items.d: The sports of swimming under the sea, with bottles to help you breathe.
items.s: California is a great place for ___ in the sea.
items.hintDe: Sport: tief unter der Meeresoberfläche schwimmen, mit Luftflaschen.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'Don't worry about your broken ankle. I'm going to take care of you' — 'broken/ankle' untaught, so changed to 'your leg' (in-bank). d avoids 'someone' using 'somebody/something' (in-bank).

items.d: To look after somebody and keep them well.
items.s: Don't worry about your leg. I'm going to ___ you.
items.hintDe: Auf jemanden aufpassen und für ihn sorgen.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'He did not want to take up a new sport. He had too much to do' — 'sport'(sg.) untaught, so changed to 'skating' (in-bank g1-u11) and tail to 'He was too busy'. d avoids untaught 'hobby/activity/sport/game' with 'a new thing you like doing in your free time'; does not leak 'take'.

items.d: To start a new thing you like doing in your free time.
items.s: He did not want to ___ skating. He was too busy.
items.hintDe: Ein neues Hobby oder eine Sportart beginnen.

provenance.note: SB master example ('Life is tough at the moment. I don't have any pocket money left.') uses untaught 'moment/left'; kept the 'Life is/can be tough' frame but rebuilt the clause in-bank ('a lot of homework and no free time'). d avoids 'hard' (untaught) with 'very difficult ... strong will'.

items.d: Very difficult; it needs a lot of work and strong will.
items.s: Life can be ___ when you have a lot of homework and no free time.
items.hintDe: Richtig schwer, kostet viel Mühe.

provenance.note: Carrier from the SB master example 'Who won the tennis tournament this year?' — 'won'/'tennis' untaught, reframed to 'Our school will play in a big sports ___ next month' (sports in-bank, no gloss needed). d uses 'competition/be the best' (in-bank), avoiding untaught 'win'.

items.d: A big sports competition where many people play to be the best.
items.s: Our school will play in a big sports ___ next month.
items.hintDe: Sportwettkampf mit vielen Runden und Teilnehmern.

provenance.note: SB master example ('And I thought my new shoes were water-proof!') and the editor-letter joke both available; chose a clean rain/jacket carrier for an unambiguous blank. One-word 'waterproof' is an untaught token under the gate, so it is kept as a tier=partial accept (full answer is the hyphenated bank form).

items.d: This keeps the rain out, so what is under it stays dry.
items.s: Make sure your jacket is ___ before you go out in this rain.
items.hintDe: So bleibt im Regen alles darunter trocken.
